digest.top_line: PBS proton vs IMRT capsular contracture in post-mastectomy reconstruction: no effect size reported from source.
digest.tldr: Single study day; breast carried the only signal. A retrospective IJROBP 2025-09 comparison of PBS proton vs IMRT in post-mastectomy pts has no extractable effect sizes from the submitted source, limiting synthesis.

sites.intro: Proton vs photon in post-mastectomy reconstruction remains an open clinical question; today's source yielded no quantitative signal.

studies.name: PBS Proton vs IMRT Capsular Contracture (post-mastectomy)
studies.tldr: PBS proton vs IMRT capsular contracture risk in post-mastectomy reconstruction: no effect size reported in source.
details: 🔍 Retrospective comparison of PBS proton vs IMRT in post-mastectomy pts undergoing breast reconstruction; published IJROBP 2025-09
📊 Primary question: does PBS proton carry higher capsular contracture risk vs IMRT? No effect size reported in source tweets
⚠️ Source is a single DOI citation only; no abstract, N, effect sizes, or design details available from the submitted content
❓ Radiation modality choice (proton vs photon) in reconstruction setting is an active clinical question given theoretical dose-bath differences

verdict.rationale: No design details, N, or effect sizes available in source; cannot classify on current evidence.
verdict.audience: Post-mastectomy pts undergoing breast reconstruction receiving adjuvant RT

open_questions: Does PBS proton vs IMRT differ in capsular contracture rate, and by how much?
Does implant type or reconstruction timing modify the modality effect?

papers.title: Does Pencil Beam Scanning Proton Therapy Impart a Higher Risk of Capsular Contracture when Compared with Intensity Modulated Photon Radiotherapy in the Post-Mastectomy Reconstruction Setting?
